---
title: "Hands-on 3"
author: "Laia LĂłpez (laia.lopez10@estudiant.upf.edu)"
date: "Last update: `15/03/2026`"      
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    fig_caption: true
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```


```{r chunk1, load_libraries, echo=TRUE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
if (!require("caret")){install.packages("caret"); require(caret)} 
if (!require("randomForest")){install.packages("randomForest"); require(randomForest)}
if (!require("pROC")){install.packages("pROC"); require(pROC)}
if (!require("recipes")){install.packages("recipes"); require(recipes)}

### Load required packages
library(readxl)
library(caret)
library(recipes)
library(randomForest)
library(pROC)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

### Read the data
df_metadata <- readxl::read_xlsx("Data/Raw_Data/Table-S1.Additional-Demographical-and-Baseline-Characteristics-of-COVID-19-Patients-and-Control-Groups.xlsx", sheet = 2)

# Rename important columns
df_metadata <- df_metadata %>%
  rename(
    Patient_ID = `Patient ID a`,
    Group = `Group d`
  )

# Create variable severity (2-3)
df_metadata <- df_metadata %>%
  mutate(
    Patient_ID = trimws(as.character(Patient_ID)),
    Group = as.numeric(Group)
  ) %>%
  filter(Group %in% c(2,3)) %>%
  mutate(
    Severity = case_when(
      Group == 2 ~ "Non-severe",
      Group == 3 ~ "Severe"
    ),
    Severity = factor(Severity, 
                      levels = c("Non-severe","Severe"))
  ) %>%
  select(Patient_ID, Severity)

```


```{r chunk2, load_libraries, echo=TRUE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# In the paper they used 18 non-severe and 13 severe patients for training data

df_proteins <- readxl::read_xlsx("Data/Raw_Data/S3-Protein_and_Metabolite_Matrix.xlsx", sheet = 2, skip = 1)

# Clean IDs
df_proteins$...1 <- trimws(as.character(df_proteins$...1))

# Remove descriptive columns
df_proteins_clean <- df_proteins[, -c(1:2)]
rownames(df_proteins_clean) <- df_proteins$...1

# Transpose the matrix
training_data <-  as.data.frame(t(df_proteins_clean))
rownames(training_data) <- make.names(rownames(training_data), unique = TRUE)

# Confirm ID match
common_ids <- intersect(df_metadata$Patient_ID, rownames(training_data))
df_metadata <- df_metadata %>% filter(Patient_ID %in% common_ids)
training_data <- training_data[match(df_metadata$Patient_ID, rownames(training_data)), , drop = FALSE]  

# Add Patient_ID as column
training_data <- tibble::rownames_to_column(training_data, var = "Patient_ID")

# Merge metadata with protein data
df_model <- inner_join(df_metadata, training_data, by = "Patient_ID")
rownames(df_model) <- df_model$Patient_ID
df_model <- df_model[,-1]

#Pre-processing from the study:
# compounds with over 80% missing ratios in a particular patient group were removed for the metabolomics dataset
#Metabolites and therapeutic compounds with over 80% missing ratios in a particular patient group were removed
#Missing values were imputed with the minimal value and zero in metabolomics and proteomics dataset respectively.

#Cols in our df
outcome_col <- 1
protein_cols <- 2:792
metab_cols   <- 793:1639

df_model[, protein_cols] <- lapply(df_model[, protein_cols], function(x) as.numeric(as.character(x)))

df_model <- df_model %>%
  select(-all_of(metab_cols))

#Proteins missing values, 0 imputation
df_model[, protein_cols] <- lapply(df_model[, protein_cols], function(x) {
  x[is.na(x)] <- 0
  x
})

```
# Exercise 1

**Build a Random Forest model to classify COVID-19 severity**

Using the training cohort provided in Supplementary Table 3, build a Random Forest classification model similar to the one in the publication to distinguish between non-Severe and Severe COVID-19 patients.

Report the following performance metrics from cross-validation:

-   Confusion matrix\
-   Accuracy\
-   ROC curve and AUC

How well does the Random Forest model separate non-Severe from Severe patients based on proteomic profiles?

```{r chunk3, load_libraries, echo=TRUE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, fig.align='center', results='hide'}
# In the paper they used 18 non-severe and 13 severe patients for training data

# Before train the model (because caret convert the class names to column names when it calculates probabilities)
df_model$Severity <- as.character(df_model$Severity)

df_model$Severity[df_model$Severity == "Non-severe"] <- "Non.severe"

df_model$Severity <- factor(df_model$Severity,
                            levels = c("Non.severe", "Severe"))

# Model configuration
set.seed(123)
rf_recipe <- recipe(Severity ~ ., data = df_model)
ctrl <- trainControl(
  method = "cv",
  number = 10,
  classProbs = TRUE, # necessary for ROC
  summaryFunction = twoClassSummary, # calculate ROC, Sens, Spec
  savePredictions = "final"
)

p <- ncol(df_model) - 1 # Max numb of variables to try for RF, exclude classification
mtry_grid <- data.frame( mtry = c(1, 2, floor(sqrt(p)), floor(p/3), p) )

# Train of RF
rf_model <- train(
  rf_recipe,
  data = df_model,
  method = "rf",
  trControl = ctrl,
  tuneGrid = mtry_grid,
  ntree = 1000, #Number of trees built in the research
  importance = TRUE,
  metric = "ROC")


rf_model
plot(rf_model)

# Save the file
png("Data/Processed_Data/rf_model_plot.png", width = 800, height = 600)
plot(rf_model)    
invisible(dev.off())

# Extract best CV predictions
best_mtry <- rf_model$bestTune$mtry
predictions <- rf_model$pred
predictions <- predictions[predictions$mtry == best_mtry, ]


# Confusion Matrix
conf_mat <- confusionMatrix(
  predictions$pred,
  predictions$obs,
  positive = "Severe"
)
conf_mat$table


# Accuracy
accuracy <- conf_mat$overall["Accuracy"]
conf_mat$overall["Accuracy"]

#ROC and AUC

roc_obj <- roc(
  predictions$obs,
  predictions$Severe
)

auc_value <- auc(roc_obj)
auc_value

plot(
  roc_obj,
  print.auc = TRUE,
  col = "blue",
  main = "ROC Curve - Random Forest",
  legacy.axes = TRUE,
  xlab = "FPR (1 - Specificity)"
)

# Save the plot
png("Data/Processed_Data/roc_curve.png", width = 800, height = 600)
plot(
  roc_obj,
  print.auc = TRUE,
  col = "blue",
  main = "ROC Curve - Random Forest",
  legacy.axes = TRUE,
  xlab = "FPR (1 - Specificity)"
)
invisible(dev.off())

```

- The model shows excellent discrimination between severe and non-severe COVID-19 patients based on proteomic profiles, suggesting that proteomic signatures strongly reflect disease severity. 
We see that the best tune corresponds to the model including 28 proteomic values, this model has a 0.85 Sensibility and Specificity. Is able to identify Severe cases correctly in most of the cases, as it can be observed in the Confusion matrix. The quality of the model gets ratified by a high AUC value. 


# Exercise 2

**Identify and interpret the most important features**

Using the trained Random Forest model from Exercise 1, identify the 25 most important protein features contributing to the classification. Disccus their biological relevance.

Compare your selected features to the proteins reported by the authors in Supplementary Table 5.

```{r chunk4, load_libraries, echo=TRUE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, fig.align='center'}

df2_proteins <- readxl::read_xlsx("Data/Raw_Data/S5-Protein_and_Metabolite_Matrix.xlsx", sheet = 2, skip = 1)

# Variable importance
var_imp <- varImp(rf_model, scale = TRUE)

# Convert to data frame
importance_df <- var_imp$importance
importance_df$Protein <- rownames(importance_df)

# Create Overall column as a mean of both classes 
importance_df$Overall <- rowMeans(importance_df[, 1:2])

# Sort by Overall
importance_df <- importance_df %>%
  arrange(desc(Overall))

# Top 25
top25_proteins <- importance_df[1:25, ] #Selecting the number of proteins used in the model

# Compare with Supplementary Table 5 to know which coincide
top25_proteins$Match <- ifelse(top25_proteins$Protein %in% df2_proteins[[1]], "Reported", "Not reported")

top25_proteins

top25 <- ggplot(top25_proteins, aes(x = reorder(Protein, Overall), y = Overall, fill = Match)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Reported" = "#B6D9B6", "Not reported" = "#E0ABB4")) +
  labs(title = "Top 25 proteins by RF importance", x = "Protein ID/Metabolite", y = "Importance")

top25

ggsave("Data/Processed_Data/top25.png", plot = top25, width = 15, height = 12, dpi = 300)

```

- We can see that most of the features selected for our model match the ones selected in the author's model, except for: P01009, P05156, P33908, P02763, P35542, P04004, P01019. 
Nevertheless, the original research includes the metabolic parameters in their study, as a result some variation is expected. 


# Exercise 3

**External prediction on an independent test cohort**

Using the final Random Forest model trained on the full training cohort, predict disease severity for patients in the independent test cohort provided in Supplementary Table 4.

Your task is to:

-   Apply the trained model to the test cohort.  
-   Obtain predicted probabilities of Severe disease for each patient.  
-   Assign predicted class labels based on these probabilities.  
-   Compare predictions to the true clinical labels.  


How does the model perform on the independent test cohort?

```{r chunk5, load_libraries, echo=TRUE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, fig.align='center'}

# Read the test data
test_df <- readxl::read_xlsx("Data/Raw_Data/S4-Independent_Test_Cohort.xlsx", sheet = 2, skip = 1)

# Clean Patient_ID
test_df$...1 <- trimws(as.character(test_df$...1))

# Remove descriptive columns
test_clean <- test_df[, -c(1:2)]  
rownames(test_clean) <- test_df$...1

# Transpose the matrix
test_data <- as.data.frame(t(test_clean))
rownames(test_data) <- make.names(rownames(test_data), unique = TRUE)

# Add Patient_ID
test_data <- tibble::rownames_to_column(test_data, var = "Patient_ID")


# Read the metadata
df_test_metadata <- readxl::read_xlsx("Data/Raw_Data/Table-S1.Additional-Demographical-and-Baseline-Characteristics-of-COVID-19-Patients-and-Control-Groups.xlsx", sheet = 2)

df_test_metadata <- df_test_metadata %>%
  rename(
    Patient_ID = `Patient ID a`,
    Group = `Group d`
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Patient_ID = trimws(as.character(Patient_ID)),
    Group = as.numeric(Group)
  ) %>%
  filter(Group %in% c(2,3)) %>%
  mutate(
  Severity = case_when(
      Group == 2 ~ "Non.severe",
      Group == 3 ~ "Severe"
    ),
    Severity = factor(Severity,
                      levels = c("Non.severe","Severe"))
  )


# Join metadata with proteins
df_test <- inner_join(df_test_metadata, test_data, by = "Patient_ID")

rownames(df_test) <- df_test$Patient_ID
df_test <- df_test[,-1]

#Identify features common with training model
common_features <- intersect(colnames(df_model)[-1], colnames(df_test))

# Subset both datasets to common features
df_model_reduced <- df_model[, c("Severity", common_features)]
df_test_final <- df_test[, c("Severity", common_features)]

# Convert to numeric and impute NA 
df_test_final[, -1] <- lapply(df_test_final[, -1], function(x) as.numeric(as.character(x)))
df_test_final[is.na(df_test_final)] <- 0


# Train the model with reduced features
set.seed(123)
rf_recipe <- recipe(Severity ~ ., data = df_model_reduced)
ctrl <- trainControl(
  method = "cv",
  number = 10,
  classProbs = TRUE,
  summaryFunction = twoClassSummary,
  savePredictions = "final"
)

p <- ncol(df_model_reduced) - 1
mtry_grid <- data.frame(mtry = c(1,2,floor(sqrt(p)), floor(p/3), p))

rf_model_reduced <- train(
  rf_recipe,
  data = df_model_reduced,
  method = "rf",
  trControl = ctrl,
  tuneGrid = mtry_grid,
  ntree = 1000,
  importance = TRUE,
  metric = "ROC"
)
```

- The Random Forest model was initially developed using the full training dataset. For external validation, only the features shared between the training and independent test cohorts were kept. As the test dataset did not contain all the feautres present in the training dataset, a final model was then re-trained on the training data using this reduced feature set to ensure compatibility with the test cohort.</b>



```{r, load_libraries, echo=TRUE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, fig.align='center'}

# Predict on test cohort
test_probs <- predict(rf_model_reduced, df_test_final, type = "prob")
test_pred  <- predict(rf_model_reduced, df_test_final)

df_test_final$Predicted_Class <- test_pred
df_test_final$Prob_Severe <- test_probs$Severe

results_table <- data.frame(
  Sample = rownames(df_test_final),
  True_Severity = df_test_final$Severity,
  Predicted_Class = df_test_final$Predicted_Class,
  Prob_Severe = df_test_final$Prob_Severe
)

# Evaluate
conf_mat_test <- confusionMatrix(
  df_test_final$Predicted_Class,
  df_test_final$Severity,
  positive = "Severe"
)

accuracy_test <- conf_mat_test$overall["Accuracy"]

roc_test <- roc(df_test_final$Severity, df_test_final$Prob_Severe)
auc_test <- auc(roc_test)

# Plot ROC
plot(roc_test,
     print.auc = TRUE,
     col = "red",
     main = "ROC Curve - External Test Cohort")

# Save the plot
png("Data/Processed_Data/ROC_test_EXT.png", width = 1200, height = 900)

plot(roc_test,
     print.auc = TRUE,
     col = "red",
     main = "ROC Curve - External Test Cohort")


# Print results
conf_mat_test
accuracy_test
auc_test

cat("============ Predicted probabilties ============ ")
results_table

```

The model achieved an AUC of 0.792 on the independent test cohort, indicating good diagnostic/predictive performance.
Although slightly lower than the performance observed in the training data, this decrease is expected and reflects a more realistic evaluation of the modelâ€™s generalization ability.
Overall, the model demonstrates robust predictive capacity when applied to the test data.


# session info {.unnumbered}

```{r, results='asis',  echo=FALSE, message=FALSE }
sessionInfo()
```
